Empires & Puzzles Snowball Review: The Mighty Pet Damage Amplifier

Don't let the eyepatch fool you; this stray cat isn't here for treats. Snowball brings one of the rarest and most potent debuffs in the game: a massive +40% damage increase from ALL sources. While his stats have aged like milk, his utility remains top-tier for Titan slayers. Read our full review to see why this kitty still has claws.
Snowball: The Cat That Makes Titans Bleed
Snowball, The Feral Furball: Damage Amplification and Fiendish Fun
Snowball is a 5-star Legendary Nature hero from the

family, originally released in May 2023. He’s a "Hit-3" Ranger who specializes in summoning fiends that act as walking targets. While he was once a terrifying attacker in PvP, the relentless march of power creep has shifted his role significantly. Today, he is less of a nuker and more of a specialized support unit, prized for a unique debuff that makes enemies take significantly more damage from everything you throw at them. He’s the cat you bring when you want to see big numbers pop up on the screen—just not necessarily from his own hits.

Snowball is a "Clawful Stray Cat" with a rugged, battle-hardened look that screams "I've seen some things." Sporting a green eyepatch, a quiver full of arrows, and a bow that looks slightly too large for him, he perfectly captures the whimsical yet dangerous aesthetic of the Mighty Pets family. He looks like he just walked out of a medieval tavern brawl and is ready to start another one. The art is detailed and full of character, a stark contrast to the often overly serious high-fantasy designs of classic heroes.
When we look at Snowball's stats in the harsh light of 2026, the reality of power creep is undeniable.
While respectable in 2023, modern attackers are pushing 1300+ base attack. Snowball hits like a wet noodle compared to the new gods of E&P.

These survivability stats are now considered low. Modern 5-stars often start with 2200+ health. Snowball is fragile and will crumble quickly under the pressure of modern special skills if not protected.
This is significantly below the current 1250+ standard for new heroes.
However, do not let this dishearten you. Snowball's value does not lie in his raw stats, but in what he enables
The crown jewel of Snowball's kit is the secondary effect of his

"The Barbed Arrow Fiend increases the damage dealt to its target from all sources by +40%." This is huge. Unlike standard defense down, this is a separate multiplier that stacks with defense down, elemental defense down, and increased special skill damage.
This includes tile damage, special skills, DoT (burn/poison), and even items. This makes him incredibly potent against Titans and in Challenge Events where maximizing damage per turn is the goal.
As long as the Fiend is alive, the debuff exists. The enemy
heal the fiend away to remove the effect. In bloody battles or against teams with poor healing, this is a death sentence.
Being Fast allows him to apply this debuff quickly, which is crucial for setting up a burst turn.
Let's break down the math behind the kitty's claws.
With +29 Magic Troop/Styx Troop: 6 tiles (Ghosted) or 7 tiles (Standard).
: Gains 5% mana when an ally summons a minion. This can theoretically allow him to charge in fewer tiles if paired with fast minion summoners (e.g., Hulda, Freya).
With Snowball's Fiend (+40% Damage Taken): ~1400 damage alone.
The calculation is multiplicative in effectiveness. You are hitting a softer target with a harder hit. This is why he is a Titan staple.
Attack: 43% of Snowball's attack. With ~1000 attack (emblems + troops), that's ~430 attack power per turn per fiend. Not game-breaking, but annoying.
Healing Absorption: 28% of target's max HP. On a modern hero with 2500 HP, the fiend absorbs ~700 healing before disappearing. This is a significant healing block.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Mighty Pets family brings some interesting synergy, particularly regarding minions and fiends.
The family bonus provides stat buffs/nerfs to minions and fiends.
Ally minions get +2%/3%/4% HP and Attack.
Enemy fiends get -2%/-3%/-4% Attack and Healing Absorption.
Nice to have, but rarely a deciding factor in a match unless you are running a full Mighty Pets team.
Deals 85% damage to all enemies when a

is summoned to an enemy. Since Snowball summons fiends himself, this triggers on his own special skill! This effectively adds a small "Hit All" component to his "Hit 3" skill, popping minions or softening up corners.
Gains 5% Health and Mana when a
is summoned to an ally. This is excellent for sustainability and mana speed if you pair him with minion summoners like the Three Kingdoms heroes or classic minion makers.
For the first 6 turns of battle, Snowball is immune to status ailments.

This is fantastic for a support/utility hero. It ensures he doesn't get silenced, blinded, or mana-slowed before he can fire his crucial special skill. In Rush wars or against fast ailment dealers, this guarantees he gets his job done.
Upon release in 2023, Snowball was a top-tier flank. His speed, damage, and unique debuff made him a menace in defense and offense. He was a shiny new toy that everyone wanted.

In 2026, Snowball has been severely power-crept in terms of stats. His direct damage (250%) is now laughable compared to heroes doing 450%+ to all at fast speed. He cannot compete as a primary damage dealer.
He is too fragile for top 100 play. He dies to splash damage before he can fire.
He remains one of the best heroes for Green Titan teams. The +40% damage amplification is rare and stacks with almost everything. He is a key component for high scores.
The damage amp helps melt bosses quickly.
Current review focuses on the base hero as no costume information was provided or is currently widely available for this specific analysis context.
, which allows him to bypass defensive buffs (like counterattack or defense up) with his
Maximizes his tile damage for Titans, which is his primary use case in 2026. The
talent also helps tiles bypass defensive buffs on mythic titans or events.
Keeps him alive longer to fire his special skill in PvP environments.
Sacrifices tile damage, which is his main asset against Titans.
For most players still using Snowball, you are likely using him for
is the only logical choice. You want every tile to hit as hard as possible under that +40% amplification window.
The +40% damage taken from all sources is a rare and powerful multiplier.
Can keep the debuff active almost permanently with decent boards.
Essential for maximizing damage scores on Blue Titans.
Self-triggers a "Hit All" passive and gains mana from ally minions.
6 turns of immunity ensures he fires at least once.
Low defense and health make him a glass cannon without the cannon part (in terms of direct skill damage).
While the effect is uncleansable, it is removed if the Fiend is healed away. Modern healers output massive healing numbers, often instantly removing the fiend and the debuff.
In a meta dominated by "Hit-All" heroes, hitting only 3 targets feels restrictive for a 5-star.
His absolute best role. Pair him with Franz, Tarlak/Miki, and Defense Down heroes for astronomical scores.
Essential for the same reasons as standard Titans.
Healing is disabled, meaning the Fiends (and the damage amp) cannot be removed by healing. They stick around until the target dies.
His fast speed and Ranger pierce can be useful, though less dominant than before.
This is the "All the Multipliers" strategy. Snowball adds +40% damage taken. Evelyn reduces Elemental Defense. Frigg reduces Defense. Tarlak boosts Attack. Franz adds
+50% damage taken (Note: Check if Franz and Snowball stack. Usually, "increased damage" debuffs overwrite if they are the same icon, but Snowball's is tied to a
. Community testing suggests they might not stack if they share the same internal mechanic ID, or they might overwrite. However, Snowball's is often preferred if Franz is too slow or locked out.

Community consensus is they often overwrite or conflict. Use one or the other. Snowball is faster; Franz hits all. If they don't stack, Snowball is the "Fast" alternative to Franz for shorter fights or 3-hit focus.)
Users noted Snowball overwrites Franz/Hammertusk but is faster. So don't pair them!
Any healer that provides massive boosted health or immediate healing can wipe the fiends instantly, removing Snowball's main utility.
Heroes that prevent fiends from landing completely negate his special skill's secondary effect.
As noted, the effects likely do not stack. Do not bring them together; choose the one best suited for the fight duration.

If you pulled Snowball early, congratulations! You have a Titan specialist who will serve you for years. Focus on leveling him to 3/70 immediately. He doesn't strictly need the final ascension to do his job on lower-level Titans, but the stats help. Do not prioritize him over a solid carry healer or sniper, but he should be your first choice for a green support utility.
Snowball is a tool, not a weapon. At this stage in the game, you are likely only using him for Titans or specific events. He is

worth Limit Breaking (LB2) for the Alpha Aether unless you are competing for top 100 in Mythic Titans and need every ounce of attack stat. For general use, LB1 is sufficient to keep him alive. If you have newer heroes like the Construct or Astral Gods, Snowball belongs on the bench for wars.
"It’s a Snowball’s chance in hell this hero won’t get a 'balance adjustment' later."
- A prescient forum user predicting nerfs or adjustments.
- Initial reactions were hyped about his damage potential, which has sadly faded with time.

Community polls showed a split: many thought he was "Over Powered" at release, but a significant number saw him as just "On Par." Time has proven him to be a utility specialist rather than an OP nuker.

Snowball is the perfect example of a hero who aged gracefully into a niche. He started as a feral beast in the arena and retired as a distinguished consultant for Titan destruction. While he may no longer terrorize the diamond raids, his ability to amplify damage keeps him off the bench and in the roster for PvE content. He’s a good kitty, just don't expect him to fight the new laser-wielding gods of 2026 alone.
Keep for Titans, bench for Wars.
A deep dive into Snowball, the 5-star Nature hero from the Mighty Pets family. We analyze his stats, special skill, and why his damage amplification keeps him relevant in 2026.
